user_query: Update fathers maple kissed taco option to Birria tacos

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 3 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Slow-cook beef in a rich, spiced adobo broth until tender and shreddable. Dip corn tortillas in the top layer of the consommé, sear on a griddle, and fill with the shredded beef and melted Oaxaca cheese. Serve with a side of the flavorful consommé for dipping, topped with fresh cilantro and diced white onions.

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria provides a high-protein meal with iron and B vitamins from the slow-cooked beef. While higher in sodium and fat due to the traditional consommé and cheese, it offers deep flavor from antioxidant-rich dried chilies. Serve with plenty of fresh cilantro and onion to add micronutrients and fiber.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the 'Maple-Kissed Skillet' taco option into 'Birria Tacos' as requested. Updated the name, description, ingredients, and nutrition note to reflect the shift from a quick skillet meal to a traditional slow-cooked Mexican dish.
